results.title: Latest Marlin News - (POND) Future Outlook, Trends & Market Insights
results.content:
Overview: Indonesia's largest crypto exchange, INDODAX, finalized the migration of Marlin's POND token from the Arbitrum network to the Ethereum ERC20 standard. Deposit and withdrawal services on the old network closed on 11 May 2026 and reopened on the new ERC20 contract on 5 June 2026. Trading remained unaffected throughout the process. What this means: This is a neutral operational update for POND. It simplifies the token's infrastructure by consolidating on a widely supported standard like ERC20, potentially improving accessibility for users on INDODAX. However, it requires users to update wallet addresses to avoid loss of funds. (INDODAX)

### 2. Binance Halts POND Deposits on Select Networks (7 May 2026) [...] Overview: Binance temporarily suspended deposits and withdrawals for POND on the BNB Chain and Arbitrum networks starting 14 May 2026 for maintenance or upgrades. The exchange noted that spot trading was not impacted and services on other supported networks remained active. What this means: This is a routine, neutral event. Such suspensions are common for network upkeep but can cause short-term liquidity constraints for users on the affected chains. It underscores the importance of checking network status before making transfers. (CoinMarketCap)

### 3. Binance Flags POND with High-Risk Monitoring Tag (30 April 2026) [...] ### 1. @WhisprNews: POND's +57% DePIN Rally bullish

"📈 Ganadores de hoy en la categoría #DePIN (26-05-2026) Marlin $POND +57.42%" – @WhisprNews (3.9K followers · 26 May 2026 15:03 UTC) View original postWhat this means: This is bullish for POND because it highlights a sudden, massive outperformance within the decentralized physical infrastructure narrative. It suggests speculative capital is rotating into the token, potentially marking the start of a short-term trend.

### 2. @aigmx_agent: Binance Monitoring Tag Raises Delisting Risk bearish

# Marlin (POND) Price Prediction

Share

By CMC AI 12 June 2026 09:13AM (UTC+0)

Ask CMC AI

See POND Price Chart

TLDR

POND's path forward balances technical promise against exchange scrutiny. [...] ### 1. Exchange Scrutiny & Liquidity Risk (Bearish Impact)

Overview: Binance placed a Monitoring Tag on POND on 30 April 2026, flagging it as high-risk due to factors like low adoption and trading volume (CoinMarketCap). This often precedes delisting if concerns aren't addressed within 3–6 months. Simultaneously, exchanges like ProBit Global delisted POND in October 2025, and INDODAX completed a network migration in June 2026, which can temporarily disrupt access.

What this means: The tag directly undermines investor confidence, often triggering sell-offs—POND dropped ~9% on the news. Reduced exchange support limits liquidity and accessibility, constraining buying pressure. The risk of losing a major venue like Binance is a significant near-term overhang. [...] ### 2. Technical Adoption & Ecosystem Growth (Bullish Impact)

Overview: Marlin's core offering is Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) infrastructure for off-chain, confidential computing. Recent updates show scaling: 873 compute instances and over 20k vCPUs processing AI workloads by August 2025 (Marlin). New integrations, like a Key Management Service with Sui Network in May 2026, and educational initiatives with Binance Academy, aim to boost developer adoption.

What this means: Real usage growth translates to demand for POND tokens, which are staked to secure the network and pay for services. Increased adoption by AI and DeFi projects could create a sustainable utility-driven price floor. Successful partnerships demonstrate execution, a key for long-term revaluation.

results.title: DNREC Debuts New Larger Pool at Killens Pond Waterpark - State of Delaware News
results.content:
Killens Pond Waterpark will debut for the 2026 season on Saturday, May 23 and operate through Labor Day, Sept. 7. Fees for the waterpark will remain the same as the 2025 season, but children under 1 year old will now be free. The waterpark will be open weekends only from Memorial Day to mid-June and seven days a week until mid-August. Two sessions are offered daily from 9:30 a.m. to 1:30 p.m. and from 2 p.m. to 6 p.m. Coupon books and group rates are available. For details, visit the destateparks.com/waterpark webpage. [...] Killens Pond Waterpark will debut for the 2026 season on Saturday, May 23 and operate through Labor Day, Sept. 7. Fees for the waterpark will remain the same as the 2025 season, but children under 1 year old will now be free. The waterpark will be open weekends only from Memorial Day to mid-June and seven days a week until mid-August. Two sessions are offered daily from 9:30 a.m. to 1:30 p.m. and from 2 p.m. to 6 p.m. Coupon books and group rates are available. For details, visit the destateparks.com/waterpark webpage. [...] Keep up to date by receiving a daily digest email, around noon, of current news release posts from state agencies on news.delaware.gov.

Here you can subscribe to future news updates.

# DNREC Debuts New Larger Pool at Killens Pond Waterpark

Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control | Division of Parks and Recreation | Newsroom | Date Posted: Friday, May 22, 2026

Killens Pond Water plan expanded 48% featuring two new spray features and lily pads at the pool.

The Delaware Department of Natural Resources and Environmental Control announced the opening of the new Killens Pond Waterpark main pool that expands space and features for visitors, enhances visibility for lifeguards and provides more efficient filtration.

# News Flash

# Morses Pond 2026 Pre-season Information

Morses Pond opens for the 2026 pre-season on Monday, June 1 through Sunday, June 21.

During pre-season, the parking lot is open ONLY to Wellesley residents or non-resident season pass holders. You must provide a valid ID or season pass receipt.

Park rangers are on duty from 12 p.m. to 8 p.m. and the guardhouse and restrooms are open from 12:30 p.m. to 7:30 p.m.

Currently there are NO LIFEGUARDS on duty - swimming is at your own risk.

Full summer season operations for Morses Pond begin on Monday, June 22 and run through Sunday, August 16, 2026. [...] Season Pass Sale Extended.Residents can buy summer season passes for Morses Pond until June 21. Prices increase on June 22.

Fees for 2026 are:

To purchase season passes, visit the Recreation Department registration webpage and enter Activity #852000.

## Energy & Natural Resources [...] based on, among other things, the expectations and analysis of current market trends and opportunities of management of the Company, such forward-looking statements have been based on expectations, factors and assumptions concerning future events which may prove to be inaccurate and are subject to numerous risks and uncertainties, certain of which are beyond the Company's control, including, but not limited to, potential operational inefficiencies due to the change in management and as a result of the SalesCloser transaction and the Viral Loops divestiture, the ability of the Company to successfully comply with the terms and conditions of the Forbearance Agreement and its other credit facilities, the adequacy of any of the Company's credit facilities or working capital to provide the [...] Mr. Gutierrez added, "Following these transactions, Wishpond enters the balance of 2026 with a clearer mandate: improve execution in the core business, strengthen customer retention, advance our AI-enabled product capabilities and operate with greater financial discipline. At the same time, Wishpond shareholders continue to participate in SalesCloser's future through our approximately 63.3% ownership interest in SalesCloser Technologies Ltd., a separately listed AI sales agent company with its own management team, capital structure and growth strategy."

results.title: Fishing in the Northern Neck - News on the Neck
results.content:
Don’t forget the topwater baits. A hollow bellied frog is great in the lily pads, along the edges, and over laydowns. A walking style bait will draw fish up and from a distance as will a buzzbait. Work them along the edges of the lily pads and over laydowns. Also work them along the top of the drop off. Just be ready for that monster bass to explode on it.

Event Reminders

Don’t forget about the meeting May 28, 2026, from 5–7 p.m. at the Callao Fire Department with the Virginia Department of Wildlife Resources (DWR). This is your opportunity to review and comment on several potential plans for Gardy’s Mill Pond and associated dam. [...] Don’t forget the topwater baits.
